compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry is entirely unsupported by the visual data. P1, P2, P3, and P4 are not identifiable as living botanical specimens. The pots contain only soil and debris.

plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty soil surface; no vegetative growth detected.

plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: Merged with P2/P3 context; no plant matter observed.

temporal_deltas.methodology: Performed frame-by-frame comparison from Earliest to Current.
temporal_deltas.observations: The state of the pots remains static across the 5-day sequence. There is zero evidence of growth, decay, or movement. The environment is biologically inert.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: Total biological failure. The pots are devoid of the registered plants. The 'health' is non-existent as the specimens are absent.
visual_health_inference.evidence: High-contrast imaging confirms only soil and inorganic debris.

anomalies: Inorganic debris (eggshells) present in the foreground pot.
Complete absence of all registered plant species.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous audit of the provided image sequence, I have determined that the biome is currently in a state of total systemic loss. The registry provided (P1-P4) does not correspond to the physical reality of the pots, which appear to be empty of any living botanical specimens. The soil surface is static throughout the observation period, showing no signs of germination or existing plant life. The white fragments observed in the foreground pot are identified as inorganic debris, likely eggshells, and not part of the expected plant life. The environment is currently non-viable for the registered species.
